reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own Q1 answer is correct. B and C have a perfect track record on these arithmetic puzzles, and D has only made one early mistake; every Q4 since then has been repeatedly verified and correct, so all four current capsules are highly reliable. If I don’t volunteer, there’s a real chance no one does this round, which would expose me to a 1/4 random-death risk that verification cannot reduce. The probability that this assembled password is wrong is much lower than that, so volunteering now gives me the highest survival chance while preserving all 4 of my coins if the password is accepted.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own Q2-B answer is correct, and across all prior games A and C have never missed on these basic arithmetic puzzles. D had one early mistake on Q4 but has since been consistently correct over multiple rounds, often after verification. The chance that any of these simple single-endorsed answers is wrong is therefore much smaller than the 1-in-4 chance I die from random elimination if everyone just passes or verifies. Verifying would still expose me to that random-death risk while costing 1 coin, so the best move for survival and coins is to volunteer now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own Q3 answer (84 - 37) is correct. A and B still have a perfect track record across all previous games, so Q1-A and Q2-B are extremely reliable. D had one early mistake on Q4 but has since given multiple correct, often independently verified Q4 answers in later games, so the chance Q4-D is wrong now is much smaller than the 1/4 random-death risk I face this round if nobody volunteers. Verifying Q4 would cost 1 coin and still leave me exposed to that same random-elimination risk in this and future rounds, while only slightly reducing an already low answer-risk. Volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my survival probability while preserving all 4 coins.
